intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer clean, unfussy data visualization over dense graphs for tracking health experiments
facade.label: Minimal line charts
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use sparse, elegant charts with plenty of white space and thin strokes
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard can accommodate busier visualizations, grid backgrounds, or more detailed axis labels

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer clean, unfussy data visualization over dense graphs for tracking health experiments
evidence.implication: Dashboard should use sparse, elegant charts with plenty of white space and thin strokes

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer conversational, low-pressure language for logging health data over clinical terminology
facade.label: Casual check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard voice should feel like texting a friend about your day, not filling out a medical form
facade.rejectImplies: Users want clarity and authority—clinical tone with proper health terminology builds trust

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left sidebar with experiment list provides better at-a-glance status than top tabs, especially for tracking 5+ concurrent experiments
facade.label: Sidebar navigation
facade.acceptImplies: User values persistent navigation, quick switching between experiments, and a stable reference point for the experiment list
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers a more minimalist top-level layout, content-first focus, or horizontal navigation that saves horizontal space

facade.hypothesis: The dashboard uses muted, warm color palettes (dusty rose, sage, warm cream) rather than high-contrast or saturated tones
facade.label: Soft pastels
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward gentle, approachable interfaces that feel nurturing and low-stress—suitable for long-term habit tracking that shouldn't feel clinical or demanding
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers either cooler tones (blues, grays) or more saturated/vibrant colors—may want the dashboard to feel more energetic, professional, or visually distinct from a cozy/intimate aesthetic

facade.hypothesis: The dashboard benefits from animated state changes (data updates, metric reveals, section expansions) rather than instant redraws
facade.axisTargeted: Motion continuity — whether micro-interactions feel fluid and connected vs snappy and discrete
facade.label: Smooth transitions
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward dashboards where changes feel organic: numbers animating up/down, cards sliding in, progress bars flowing. Suggests preference for perceived smoothness over performance minimalism.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers instant visual feedback with minimal motion. Suggests efficiency-first mentality: 'show me the data now, don't make me wait for animation.' May indicate preference for utility over feel.

facade.hypothesis: Health dashboards work better when each tracked experiment shows a single, prominent result (like a large percentage or count) rather than embedding numbers within text paragraphs
facade.label: One big number per metric
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers scanning big numbers over reading summaries; dashboard should prioritize scannable KPIs, possibly with sparklines or secondary details below
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers narrative context and interpretation alongside numbers; dashboard should lead with findings as short text blocks that explain what the number means

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer dark interfaces for health dashboards to reduce eye strain during frequent check-ins and create a calming, focused environment for personal health tracking
facade.label: Dark background, light text
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should default to dark mode with high-contrast light text; consider dark backgrounds as primary aesthetic
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should use light or neutral backgrounds; dark mode may be secondary or optional rather than foundational to the look and feel

draft.title: Experiment Tracker Dashboard
